title: alsophila confinis
intro: Alsophila confinis, a species of flowering plant in the family Cyathea, is commonly known as a fern. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea, and it has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of Alsophila confinis have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Alsophila confinis can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, or by making a decoction, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
